zh.lifeSummary: 當William Alston of Stristed於1307年出生在英國英格蘭艾可斯迪斯泰德時，他的父親John Alston20歲，母親Ann Symons19歲。他於1332年與Mrs. William Stristed Alston在英國英格蘭艾可斯迪斯泰德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1375年在英國艾可斯迪斯泰德享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Alston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Alstan, which is a coalescence of several different Old English personal names: Æthelstān ‘noble stone’, Ælfstān ‘elf stone’, Ealdstān ‘old stone’, or Ealhstān ‘temple stone’.

habitational name from any of various places called Alston (in Cumbria, Lancashire, Devon, and Somerset) or Alstone (in Gloucestershire and Staffordshire). With the exception of Alston in Cumbria, which is formed with the Old Norse personal name Halfdan, these placenames all consist of an Old English personal name + Old English tūn ‘settlement’, for example Ælfsige in the case of Alstone in Gloucestershire.

variants.name: Aston
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Ad(e)stan, which is usually from Old English Æthelstān (see Alston ). It may also be from the much rarer Old English name Ēadstān.
habitational name from any of several places in England called Aston. Most were named with Old English ēast ‘east’ + tūn ‘settlement’. In a few cases the first element is æsc ‘ash tree’.

variants.name: Hailstone
meanings: habitational name from Hailstone Hill in Wiltshire or Hailstone Farm in Gloucestershire, the former of which may be derived from Old English hālig-stān ‘holy stone’ or hagolstān ‘hailstone’ (Middle English hawelston, hailston).

from Ailstan, a variant of the Old English personal name Æthelstān (‘noble’ + ‘stone’), with prosthetic initial H-. The first element of the Old English name developed from Æthel- to Ægel- and then to Ail-; it was often further shortened to Al- (see Alston ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William Alston of Stristed was born in 1307, in Stisted, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Alston, was 20 and his mother, Ann Symons, was 19. He married Mrs. William Stristed Alston in 1332, in Stisted, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1375, in Stisted, Essex, England, at the age of 68.

Il a épousé Mrs. William Stristed Alston en 1332, à Stisted, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1375, à Stisted, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Alston of Stristed(이)가 1307년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 스티스테드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Alston(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Symons(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1332년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 스티스테드에서 Mrs. William Stristed Alston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1375년 영국 에세스 스티스테드에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.
